description:
You are a Logistics Analyst at a regional freight consolidator. The operations team suspects certain carriers have systemic delay issues—patterns of 3+ consecutive late shipments on specific routes—and want data before contract renegotiations. Analyze 6 months of shipment data in the LOGISTICS schema (connected as logistics_analyst / Logistics2024 to XEPDB1; Host=localhost, Port=1521). 

Detect carriers with 3 or more consecutive late shipments (actual_delivery > expected_delivery) on the same route by creating a view CONSECUTIVE_DELAY_PATTERNS using the MATCH_RECOGNIZE clause, ordered by ship_date and partitioned by carrier_id and route_id, including columns carrier_id, route_id, and consecutive_late_count. Create a view CARRIER_SCORECARDS calculating per-carrier rolling 30-day shipment counts using a range interval of 30 days, including carrier_id and ship_date. Identify routes where average actual transit time exceeds SLA transit time (sla_hours) by more than 20% in a view ROUTE_BOTTLENECK_ANALYSIS with columns route_id, origin_city, dest_city, sla_hours, and avg_actual_transit_hours. Populate a new table AT_RISK_SHIPMENTS (alert_id, shipment_id, carrier_id, route_id) using a procedure PROC_FLAG_AT_RISK_SHIPMENTS that identifies 'IN_TRANSIT' shipments where the carrier-route historical on-time rate is below 70%. Create a view CARRIER_COMPARISON_PIVOT using the PIVOT clause to cross-tabulate carrier names against total shipment counts. Export a summary report containing carrier names and total shipment counts to /home/ga/carrier_performance.csv.
